characters.name: Denise Dove
characters.location: 14 Sycamore Ln, Lot 7 (meter box, upper ledge)
characters.key_facts: Petitioner 007B. Pro se. Two eggs. Sealed nest in 40 min with pre-stashed material. Sycamore Lane Perch regular. Protective of nest at 14. "How far from 14." [Perch: #sycamore-lane, #garden-boundary, #elm-row]

characters.name: Greg Dove
characters.key_facts: Respondent 007B. Pro se. Left Jan 27. Gone 11 days. Observed near 22 Birch Ct 4x. Has been in a drainage pipe. Claims zip tie.

characters.name: Cheryl Sparrow
characters.key_facts: Witness for Denise. Neighborhood watch. Timed Greg's departure. Foraging: rotating schedule, Birch Ct on Fridays. Filed 6 extra pages. Drives Sycamore Lane Perch channels. "Just for context" is her verbal tic. Narrates cats, cars, bread, dogs in real-time. [Perch: #sycamore-lane, #sycamore-east, #garden-boundary, #elm-row]

characters.name: Linda Sparrow
characters.location: 22 Birch Court (~0.3 mi east of 14 Sycamore)
characters.key_facts: Never appears. Greg observed at/near her address 4x. Has a balcony. Greg brought vine there. Species: "Sparrow" is canonical (published docket).

characters.name: Judge Miriam Owl (Hon. M. Owl)
characters.key_facts: Presiding judge. Appointed 2019.

characters.name: T. Nuthatch
characters.key_facts: Clerk of Court.

characters.name: R. Wren
characters.key_facts: Deputy Clerk.

characters.name: Dennis
characters.key_facts: No surname given. "Between situations." Bench: 5:30–7:15 PM. Wife filed 014B citing "has been on a bench." Default ruling: wife gets nest, Dennis gets bench. Answering machine: 14 messages, 13 from one number. Man feeds pigeons 6 feet from him weekly; he faces the water. "It did not seem like something to say." [Perch: #bench-row, #south-path-and-park, others]

characters.name: E. Starling
characters.key_facts: Dennis's bench companion. "Not a situation." Appeared on bench Coo #09. Wrote letter clarifying non-situation Coo #10. Personal ad that isn't one Coo #12. "The bread was fine." Appears in ~5 Perch channels. [Perch: #municipal-notices, #bench-row, #south-path-and-park, others]

characters.name: Conrad
characters.key_facts: Morning singer. "In recovery." Quiet hours ordered: 9PM–6:30AM (Ord. 9.3(a)). Sang at 6:34 AM (technical compliance). 6:29 AM potential violation under review. Most ubiquitous Perch voice (~9 channels). Principled objector. "I am not a display." Crosses between lane and park crews. [Perch: multiple]

characters.name: R. Nuthatch
characters.key_facts: No relation to Clerk. Filed original noise complaint re Conrad. Called 6:34 AM "a position." Roams between lane and park in Perch. Cites ordinances. [Perch: #municipal-notices, #sycamore-east, #garden-boundary, #upper-canopy, others]

characters.name: Arthur P.
characters.key_facts: Wife moved to coast Feb 2026. Reads the Coo alone. The tender moment (Issue 05). Sycamore Lane Perch regular. "Sometimes you sit in the car because the car is the last place that is just yours." [Perch: #sycamore-lane, #sycamore-east]

characters.name: Milo S.
characters.location: Dad on Sycamore, Mom on Birch Court
characters.key_facts: Child of divorced parents. Court-ordered flying schedule. Two appearances (Issues 03, 07).

characters.name: Gerald H.
characters.key_facts: Margaret H.'s ex-husband. Claims park perch since 2022. Mother also uses the perch. Sycamore Lane Perch regular. Objects to being observed, then can't stop reading. [Perch: #sycamore-lane, #sycamore-east, #garden-boundary, #elm-row]

characters.name: Margaret H.
characters.key_facts: Gerald's ex-wife. Wrote letter Issue 01 (complaint about ex at perch).

characters.name: F. Lark
characters.key_facts: Observed Dennis and E. Starling on bench (Coo #09 letter). Deeper voice in The Perch. Observational, gentle, careful around the bench. Park crew lead — appears across Park Grounds and Open Grounds channels. [Perch: #south-path-and-park, #bench-row, #south-lot, #west-meadow, #upper-canopy, #culvert-path, #north-loop]

characters.name: Robin
characters.key_facts: Found Dennis "melancholy, then familiar, then honestly a little annoying" (Coo #04). Personal ad Coo #06 (female, 39 — may be same bird). Sharp voice in The Perch. Blunt, dry, says things others are thinking. Park crew lead — appears across Park Grounds and Open Grounds channels. [Perch: #south-path-and-park, #bench-row, #south-lot, #west-meadow, #upper-canopy, #culvert-path, #north-loop]

characters.name: Martin Perch
characters.key_facts: Co-founder Perch & Perch Family Law (est. 2021).

characters.name: Diane Perch
characters.key_facts: Co-founder Perch & Perch Family Law (est. 2021).

characters.name: Karen Hawk
characters.key_facts: Attorney at law. 18 years experience (as of current ads). Specializes in rapid descents.

characters.name: D.D.
characters.key_facts: Karen Hawk client testimonial. Initials suggest Denise Dove — ambiguity intentional.

characters.name: G.F.
characters.key_facts: Karen Hawk client testimonial, Issue 02.

cases.key_facts: Greg left Jan 27. Hearing concluded (Coo #06). Ruling issued (Coo #07).
"Deliberately humming" during meals. Hearing May 12, 2026 (Coo #06). Ruled: vary the rhythm (Coo #11).

cases.status: Ruled. Quiet hours 9PM–6:30AM. One potential violation under review.
cases.key_facts: Filed by Clerk on behalf of 3 Municipal Oak + 1 Sycamore residents. Hearing (Coo #09). Ruling (Coo #10). Conrad sang 6:29 AM (Coo #12).
Dennis's case. Wife filed. He didn't contest. Service confirmed via third bench. Nest to wife, bench to Dennis.

Motion to compel division of jointly held materials. Both parties requested continuance; neither stated whose request takes precedence. Set for week of May 11, 2026 (Late Light) per Spring Term Docket.

Confirmation of nest dissolution; entry of default. Petitioner C. Sparrow; respondent did not file an answer. Respondent unseen on the branch since February 2026. Set for week of May 18, 2026 (Afternoon Calendar).
Status conference re: park perch (south end). Both parties willing to attend; neither willing to leave. Set for week of April 27, 2026 (Afternoon Calendar).

Modification of visitation schedule. Caption and parties not displayed. Court has noted that scheduling difficulties between two parents are not, by themselves, grounds for an emergency. Set for week of May 25, 2026 (Emergency).

cases.parties: In re: Unclaimed Vine, Birch / Sycamore
cases.key_facts: Petition for adjudication of abandoned property. Petitioner: Clerk's Office; no respondent appearing. Vine has been at Clerk's Office since February 2026. Set for week of May 4, 2026 (Morning Sitting).

cases.parties: In re: Telephone Wire (south parking lot)
cases.key_facts: Names withheld. Petition arising from community meeting (April 21, 2026). Court has reviewed meeting transcript and notes that parties did not, in fact, dispute the wire. Set for week of May 18, 2026 (Late Light).
Not developed. Referenced as "fullest gallery since" (Coo #09).
Status update Coo #02: alternating weekends, shared Wednesdays, one floating holiday.

Fully developed as interactive proceedings page (nest-court-proceedings-pigeon). Pigeon cooing at volume after wife's departure. Noise measurements 62–71 dB (threshold 55 dB). Volume increase coincided with wife leaving (~18 months prior).

forms.name: Spring Term Docket
forms.purpose: Clerk's Office term-by-term calendar of pending matters, recent dispositions, and items on the Court's attention. Issued at the start of each term and revised periodically through Mid-Term Notices.
forms.notes: Lives at `/is/writing/nest-court/calendar/`. Spring 2026 term posted Mar 2, 2026; last revised Apr 14, 2026.
